battle_dancer.name: Battle Dancer
description: ## Battle Dancer
‹SOURCE Martial Arts Handbook/4›

Originating among the Mwangi tribes of Sargava, battle dancers practice a martial art that emphasizes constant motion, hiding their training from colonial oppressors by presenting it as an acrobatic dance. Sargava's alliance with the Shackles has led to a proliferation of the art there, and battle dancers are often found among Mwangi-descended pirates.
::cskill{gain="Perform« (all)" lose="Knowledge (dungeoneering)"}
::ab[Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="A battle dancer isn't proficient with shields." alter="The brawler's armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Dancer's Cunning (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A battle dancer can treat her Charisma score as her Intelligence score for the purpose of meeting the prerequisites of combat feats. In addition, she can attempt a Perform (dance) check instead of a Bluff check to feint in combat, and her unarmed strikes are considered to have the ‹misc/performance› weapon special quality." replace="Brawler's cunning"}

::ab[Rolling Flurry (Ex)]{icon=info ability="When a battle dancer uses her brawler's flurry, she must move 5 feet before each melee attack or combat maneuver. If she is unable to move 5 feet, she can't attempt any further attacks or combat maneuvers. She can't exceed her maximum speed. This movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity if the brawler would be able to take a 5-foot step normally; if she would be unable to (for instance, if she were in ‹rule/difficult terrain›), the movement provokes attacks of opportunity as normal unless she succeeds at the appropriate Acrobatics checks. While using this ability, she can still take her normal 5-foot step before or after making her attacks." alter="Brawler's flurry"}

::ab[Dancing Dodge (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def immediate="When an opponent attempts a melee attack against a battle dancer, the battle dancer can expend one use of an attack of opportunity to move 5 feet, granting the battle dancer a dodge bonus to AC equal to her Charisma modifier (minimum 0) against the triggering attack. The attack is still resolved normally, even if the battle dancer's movement takes her out of the triggering attack's reach. This movement is not a 5-foot step and provokes attacks of opportunity from creatures other than the one that triggered this ability, though the battle dancer can attempt an Acrobatics check to avoid provoking the attacks." useInc="2~brawler" special="She can use this ability only while wearing light armor or no armor, and while carrying no heavier than a light load." replace="AC bonus"}

::ab[Sparring Partners (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost useInc="6~brawler~4" ability="When a battle dancer gains a ‹rule/flanking› bonus on an attack while using her *rolling flurry* ability, the ally providing the flank gains a flanking bonus on its next attack against that opponent, even if the battle dancer moves out of a flanking position. If either the opponent or the ally moves more than 5 feet before the ally's attack, the flanking bonus is lost. This ability works only if the ally is on the opposite side of an opponent during the battle dancer's attack, not if a flanking bonus is gained in some other way, such as through ‹feat/Gang Up›." replace=Knockout}

bouncer.name: Bouncer
description: ## Bouncer
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Taverns/58›
Some taverns employ bouncers to keep out undesirables or for when their customers get too rowdy.
>@HL[Note:] This archetype extensively uses the optional ‹rule/Bar Fights› rules.

::ab[Crowd Control (Ex)]{icon=power passive="When in a bar fight, a bouncer has her tenacity reduced by the crowd at the end of a round by the minimum possible amount. In addition, when she takes the mix it up action, she reduces the tenacity of the crowd by an additional amount equal to her brawler level."}

::ab[Lesser Flexibility (Ex)]{icon=down ability="A bouncer can use her martial flexibility only a number of times per day equal to 2 + ‹misc/half› her brawler level (minimum 3 times per day)." alter="Martial flexibility"}

::ab[Drunk Handler (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-lower passive="A bouncer gains a +2 circumstance bonus on Intimidate and Sense Motive checks against foes that are tipsy or worse. In addition, when in a bar fight, she ignores damage reduction against nonlethal damage her targets have from being drunk or worse." replace="The bonus feat gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Drunk Knockout (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A bouncer can use one use of her *knockout* ability to attempt a similar maneuver in a bar fight against a major participant who is drunk or worse. She must declare her intent before attempting her brawling check, and if she hits and reduces the target's tenacity, that target must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw (DC = 10 + half the bouncer's brawler level + the higher of the bouncer's Strength or Dexterity modifiers) or immediately be reduced to 0 tenacity."}

::ab[Bar Fight Mastery (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power passive="In addition to the benefits of *close weapon mastery,* when a bouncer confirms a critical hit against a major participant in a bar fight, she also reduces the tenacity of the crowd by her normal brawling damage amount (this amount isn't doubled)." alter="Close weapon mastery"}

constructed_pugilist.name: Constructed Pugilist
description: ## Constructed Pugilist
‹SOURCE People of the Wastes/8›

Mutants whose mutations make using standard weapons or other gear difficult sometimes attach mechanical prostheses to their existing limbs. Many develop combat skills that use their prostheses so they always have a weapon at hand. Over time, these brawlers customize their mechanical limbs, adding additional weaponry and other mechanisms.
::cskill{gain="Craft« (weapons)"}
:::ab{title="Constructed Limb (Ex)" icon=power action=Ability}

A constructed pugilist begins play with a special prosthetic limb built for fighting and typically made of iron, steel, stone, or wood, with the corresponding hardness and hit points ([see Table 7-13](rule/breaking_things)). She treats attacks with this limb as unarmed strikes that gain all the benefits of the brawler's unarmed strike class feature. She treats the limb as a light weapon and is proficient with it. Removing or reattaching the constructed limb takes 10 minutes.

The constructed pugilist can improve her constructed limb as if it were a normal melee weapon. For the purpose of rebuilding the limb as a masterwork weapon, rebuilding the limb out of a special material, or adding certain magical special abilities to it, the limb counts as a one-handed melee weapon that costs 60 gp and weighs 6 pounds - though it is specially fitted to the constructed pugilist and can't be resold or used by anyone else. A constructed limb can benefit from either its own enhancement bonus and special abilities or those granted by an ‹magic-wondrous/amulet of mighty fists›, but if it would benefit from both simultaneously, it benefits only from the source with the higher effective enhancement bonus (the limb benefits from its own abilities in the case of a tie).

::ab[Limb Modification]{head icon=power ability="A constructed pugilist chooses one of the following limb modifications at 1st level and another at 6th, 10th, 12th, and 20th levels. These modifications do not require the constructed pugilist to rebuild her constructed limb." replace="Martial flexibility"}

::ab[Flex Limb]{next icon=melee order=ns note="The limb is divided into jointed segments held in place with rigid rods." standard="The constructed pugilist can release the rods and turn her limb into a whip-like appendage. In this mode, the prosthesis can be used to make ‹rule/trip› attempts, and it grants a +2 bonus on ‹rule/disarm› attempts made using it. The constructed pugilist doesn't run the risk of dropping her limb if she fails. Returning the limb to its rigid form takes a @HLstandard_action." special="As a @HLstandard_action, a constructed pugilist with both *flex limb* and *limb extender* can activate or deactivate one of those limb modifications or both simultaneously; when the constructed pugilist has both *flex limb* and *limb extender* activated, she gains the benefits of reach with the flex limb abilities."}

::ab[Grapnel Arm]{next icon=ranged ability="The constructed pugilist can fire a grappling hook built into her prosthesis. The grappling hook is attached to a 40-foot-long fine chain affixed to the limb. The constructed pugilist can attack with the grappling hook as a @HLstandard_action, making a ranged touch attack against the target. The grappling hook can't be used as part of a ‹rule/full attack›. On a successful hit, the grappling hook deals no damage, but it functions as though it had the ‹misc/grapple› weapon special feature, except it requires only a hit (not a critical hit) and the grapple ends if the constructed pugilist moves more than 40 feet away from the grappled creature. The constructed limb can't be used to make melee attacks until the grappling hook has been reloaded (a @HLstandard_action)."}

::ab[Limb Extender]{next icon=melee standard="The constructed pugilist can activate an extender, giving her limb the ‹misc/reach› weapon special feature. Returning the limb to its normal length is also a standard action."}

::ab[Shielding Limb]{next icon=def passive="The constructed pugilist learns to block blows with her mechanical limb. She gains a +2 shield bonus to AC, though she loses this bonus anytime she loses her Dexterity bonus to AC."}

::ab[Tight Grip]{next icon=boost-def passive="Special tensors allow the constructed pugilist to lock her grip in place. When using her constructed limb, the constructed pugilist gains a +1 bonus on Climb checks and combat maneuver checks to disarm and grapple, as well as to her CMD against attempts to disarm items held in the limb." special="This modification can be chosen only if the limb includes a hand or other gripping appendage."}

::ab[Vicious Blades]{next icon=melee-boost passive="Razor-sharp blades on the surface of the limb cause it to deal bludgeoning and slashing damage and increase its critical threat range to 19-20." special="A constructed pugilist who has the *vicious spikes* limb modification can't select this modification."}

::ab[Vicious Spikes]{next icon=melee-boost passive="Long spikes protrude from the limb, causing it to deal bludgeoning and piercing damage and increasing its critical multiplier to &times&3." special="A constructed pugilist who has the *vicious blades* limb modification can't select this modification."}

::ab[Bonus Item Creation Feats]{l=2 icon=power note="A constructed pugilist adds ‹feat/Craft Magic Arms and Armor›, ‹feat/Master Craftsman› (Craft &#91&weapons&#93&), and ‹feat/Skill Focus› (Craft &#91&weapons&#93&) to the list of bonus feats she can choose (instead of just combat feats)." alter="Bonus combat feats"}

exemplar.name: Exemplar
description: ## Exemplar
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/86›
A versatile soldier who inspires her companions with her fighting prowess, an exemplar is at home on the front lines of battles anywhere.

::ab[Call to Arms (Ex)]{icon=boost l=1 move="An exemplar can expend a use of martial flexibility to rouse her allies into action. All allies within 30 feet are no longer ‹rule/flat-footed›, even if they are surprised." imp6="The exemplar can use it as a @HLswift_action instead." imp10="She can use it as a @HLfree_action." imp12="She can use it as an @HLimmediate_action." replace="Unarmed strike~close weapon mastery"}

::ab[Inspiring Prowess (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power-boost usage="2 + brawler level + Cha modifier rounds/day" ability="An exemplar gains the ability to use certain ‹ability/bardic performances›. The exemplar's effective bard level for this ability is equal to her brawler level - 2." imp3="The exemplar can use ‹performance/inspire courage›." imp11="The exemplar can use ‹performance/inspire greatness›." imp15="The exemplar can use ‹performance/inspire heroics›." special="Instead of the Perform skill, she activates this ability with impressive flourishes and displays of martial talent (this uses visual components). This ability otherwise functions as bardic performance; feats and other effects that affect bardic performance (such as the ‹feat/Extra Performance› feat) apply to it." replace="Maneuver training~AC bonus"}

::ab[Field Instruction (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-boost standard="An exemplar can grant a ‹main/teamwork feat› to all allies within 30 feet who can see and hear her. This teamwork feat must be one the exemplar knows or has gained with the martial flexibility ability. Allies retain the use of this teamwork feat for 3 rounds + 1 round for every 2 brawler levels. If the granted teamwork feat is one gained from martial flexibility, this duration ends immediately if the exemplar loses access to that feat. Allies don't need to meet the prerequisites of this teamwork feat. This ability otherwise counts as the cavalier's tactician class feature; feats and other effects which affect tactician (such as ‹feat/Practiced Tactician›) apply to it." usage="Once a day" imp9="She can use this twice a day." imp12="She can use this three times a day." imp17="She can use this four times a day." replace="Brawler's strike"}

feral_striker.name: Feral Striker
description: ## Feral Striker
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/42›
A feral striker taps into a reservoir of druidic power hidden inside herself, allowing her to take animalistic characteristics.

::ab[Feral Aspect (Su)]{icon=power useUnit=minute useL3=brawler useNC=1 imp1="This functions as the ‹ability/shifter »aspect<s› class feature of the shifter, except the feral striker can choose only from the ‹aspect/bear›, ‹aspect/bull›, ‹aspect/falcon›, ‹aspect/snake›, ‹aspect/stag›, ‹aspect/tiger›, and ‹aspect/wolf› aspects. She can shift into an aspect as a @HLswift_action. On her turn, she may dismiss it as a @HLfree_action. She has access to only the minor versions all these aspects." imp6="The feral striker can take on two minor aspects simultaneously as a @HLmove_action. Each aspect counts toward her total number of minutes (so if a feral striker who normally has 9 total minutes of aspects takes on two aspects simultaneously for 4 minutes, she still has 1 minute remaining). She can end one or both aspects as a @HLfree_action on her turn." imp10="The feral shifter adds her Constitution modifier to the total number of minutes per day in which she can use feral aspect." imp12="The feral striker can take on three aspects simultaneously as a @HLmove_action or one or two aspects as a @HLswift_action." imp20="The feral striker can take on as many aspects as she wishes at once as a @HLswift_action, and they count as only one aspect for the purpose of calculating duration." replace="Martial flexibility"}

hinyasi.name: Hinyasi
description: ## Hinyasi
‹SOURCE Blood of the Ancients/9›

After Yamasa seceded from Lirgen, their elite combatants formed a military caste, called hinyasi, who protected Yamasa for centuries. Their martial traditions were centered on the use of farming tools and on other improvised weapons.

::ab[Humble Beginnings (Ex)]{icon=power-down choice="A hinyasi gains her choice of ‹feat/Catch Off-Guard› or ‹feat/Throw Anything› as a bonus feat." order=cn note="She is not proficient with shields." alter="The brawler's armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Improvisation Training (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A hinyasi is skilled at fighting with ‹misc/improvised weapons›. She deals damage equal to her unarmed strike damage while fighting with an improvised weapon. A hinyasi treats improvised weapons as weapons from the close ‹ability/fighter »weapon group<s›." replace="The bonus combat feat gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Improvised Maneuver (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power flavor="A hinyasi learns to confound and befuddle her opponents with improvised weapons." choice="She chooses one of the following combat maneuvers: bull rush, drag, disarm, sunder, reposition, or trip." free="When she hits an opponent with an improvised weapon, the hinyasi can perform her chosen combat maneuver against the same target. She takes a -4 penalty on the combat maneuver check, and she can't attempt an improvised maneuver check more than once per round. This combat maneuver still provokes attacks of opportunity unless the hinyasi has a feat or ability that allows her to perform it without provoking attacks of opportunity." order=cf imp10="She chooses a second combat maneuver that she can use with this ability, though she can still perform only one improvised maneuver per round." imp16="She chooses a third, though she can still perform only one improvised maneuver per round." replace=Knockout}

::ab[Improvisation Mastery (Ex)]{icon=boost choice="A hinyasi picks one type of improvised weapon (light, one-handed, or two-handed) and gains a +2 bonus on attack and damage rolls with that type of weapon." replace="Close weapon mastery"}

living_avalanche.name: Living Avalanche
description: ## Living Avalanche
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/42›
When a living avalanche is on the move, no one can stand in her way.

::ab[Earth Discipline (Ex)]{icon=power l2="A living avalanche gains ‹feat/Improved Overrun› as a bonus feat, even if she doesn't meet the prerequisites." l5="She gains ‹feat/Improved Bull Rush› as a bonus feat, even if she doesn't meet the prerequisites." replace="Bonus feats gained at 2nd and 5th levels."}

::ab[Landslide (Ex)]{icon=down-boost l3="A living avalanche must choose ‹rule/overrun› as her first combat maneuver for maneuver training." l7="She must choose ‹rule/bull rush› as her second combat maneuver for maneuver training. She doesn't gain training in any new combat maneuvers at later levels, but her bonuses on combat maneuver checks and to CMD for bull rush and overrun combat maneuvers increase as normal." l11="If she succeeds at an overrun combat maneuver as part of a ‹rule/charge›, she automatically knocks her target ‹rule/prone› (no matter the result of her check). In addition, if the living avalanche succeeds at a bull rush combat maneuver as part of a charge, she reduces the penalty combat maneuver checks to bull rush each creature beyond the first by 2." l15="The living avalanche can attempt bull rush and overrun combat maneuvers against creatures two sizes larger than herself, but only as part of a charge." l19="If the living avalanche succeeds at a bull rush combat maneuver as part of a charge, she reduces the penalty on combat maneuver checks to bull rush each creature beyond the first by an additional 2. When attempting an overrun combat maneuver as part of a charge, she also ignores any increase to a creature's CMD from having more than two legs." alter="Maneuver training"}

::ab[Unyielding (Ex)]{icon=def l4="When a living avalanche wears light armor or no armor, she gains DR 1/adamantine." l9="This becomes DR 2/adamantine." l13="This becomes DR 3/adamantine." l18="This becomes DR 4/adamantine." replace="The brawler's AC bonus"}

::ab[Avalanche (Ex)]{l=16 icon=power fullround="A living avalanche can attempt to overrun multiple creatures. The living avalanche moves up to twice her speed in a straight line and attempts an overrun combat maneuver against each creature in her path, as long as that creature is no more than two size categories larger than herself. Each creature she succeeds against is knocked prone and takes damage as if the living avalanche hit it with a close weapon she is wielding or an unarmed strike. If she fails her combat maneuver check against a creature, she stops her movement in the space directly in front of the creature." replace="Awesome blow"}

::ab[Improved Avalanche (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost ability="If a living avalanche rolls a natural 20 on an overrun combat maneuver check as part of the avalanche ability, she can immediately attempt to confirm the critical hit by attempting another combat maneuver check with the same modifiers as the previous one; if the critical hit is confirmed, the attack deals double damage." replace="Improved awesome blow"}

mutagenic_mauler.name: Mutagenic Mauler
description: ## Mutagenic Mauler
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/86›
Not content with perfecting her body with natural methods, a mutagenic mauler resorts to alchemy to unlock the primal beast within.

::ab[Mutagen (Su)]{l=1 icon=power-melee ability="A mutagenic mauler discovers how to create a mutagen that she can imbibe in order to heighten her physical prowess, though at the cost of her personality. This functions as an ‹class/alchemist«'s› mutagen and uses the brawler's class level as her alchemist level for this ability (alchemist levels stack with brawler levels for determining the effect of this ability)." special="A mutagenic mauler counts as an alchemist for the purpose of imbibing a mutagen prepared by someone else." imp6="A mutagenic mauler gains a +2 bonus on damage rolls when she attacks in melee while in her mutagenic form." imp11="The damage bonus becomes +3." imp16="The damage bonus becomes +4." imp10="A mutagenic mauler learns one of the following alchemist discoveries: ‹discovery/feral mutagen›, ‹discovery/infuse mutagen›, ‹discovery/preserve organs›, ‹discovery/spontaneous healing›." imp12="The mutagenic mauler learns the ‹discovery/greater mutagen› discovery." replace="Martial flexibility"}

::ab[Beastmorph (Su)]{icon=power-boost ability="A mutagenic mauler gains additional abilities when using her mutagen." imp4="She gains low-light vision and a +10 enhancement bonus to her base speed." imp9="She gains darkvision to a range of 30 feet and a climb speed of 15 feet." imp13="The enhancement bonus to her base speed increases to +15 feet and she gains the ‹umr/scent› ability within 30 feet." imp18="Her climb speed increases to 30 feet, and the enhancement bonus to her base speed increases to +20 feet." replace="AC bonus"}

shield_champion.name: Shield Champion
description: ## Shield Champion
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/86›

Stalwart in battle, a shield champion has perfected an entire martial discipline relying on only her hand-to-hand fighting skills and her ever-present shield. What she forgoes in weapon versatility and improved combat maneuvering, she makes up for in her ability to turn her defense into a weapon.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=info note="A shield champion is proficient with all simple weapons and with shields as weapons. She is also proficient with light armor, and with ‹eq-armor/buckler«s›, ‹eq-armor/light shield«s›, and ‹eq-armor/heavy shield«s›." replace="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Throw Shield (Ex)]{l=3 icon=ranged ability="A shield champion can throw a heavy or light shield as a normal (non-improvised) thrown weapon with a range increment of 10 feet or the shield's range increment, whichever is greater. The thrown shield deals the same damage as a ‹misc/shield bash›, and any damage increases from ‹eq-armorenh/shield spikes› apply to this attack. A shield champion is treated as having the ‹feat/Far Shot› feat for the purpose of determining range increment penalties for throwing a shield." imp7="A shield champion can use a thrown shield to perform a bull rush, dirty trick, disarm, reposition, or trip ‹rule/combat maneuver<s› as if she were making a melee shield bash attack." imp11="A shield champion gains ‹feat/Shield Master› as a bonus feat. She must meet all prerequisites before selecting that feat." replace="Maneuver training at 3rd, 7th, and 11th levels."}

:::ab{l=5 title="Returning Shield (Ex)" icon=ranged-power action=Ability imp9="A shield champion gains ‹feat/Greater Shield Focus› as a bonus feat. She must meet all prerequisites before selecting that feat." imp12="A shield champion can use her unarmed strike damage when dealing damage with a shield (whether in melee or thrown) or the shield's damage, whichever is greater." imp17="By ricocheting her shield off a nearby surface before hitting her intended target, she can treat that surface as the origin square of the attack to determine whether the target is ‹rule/flanked› and whether the target gains a ‹rule/cover› bonus to its AC. She can use this ability to attack a creature with ‹rule/total »concealment› from her if she knows what square it occupies, but the attack still has a 50% miss chance." special="A shield champion can throw a shield as part of a brawler's flurry." replace="Brawler's strike"}

A shield champion can throw a shield so it ricochets off her target (and possibly other solid objects) to return to her at the end of her turn. This ability functions whether or not the shield champion hits her opponent or moves on her turn. The shield deals no damage to targets it bounces off other than the original target of the shield champion's attack. Other circumstances can prevent the shield from returning to the shield champion, such as an opponent using a ‹rule/readied action› to catch the shield, or the shield sticking to a ‹monster/mimic«'s› adhesive. The shield champion can opt to not have a thrown shield return to her, in which case it falls to the ground as it normally would. If the shield has the ‹magic-enh/returning› weapon special ability, she can use either that or this ability.

If a shield champion has additional attacks from a high base attack bonus, these additional attacks can be ricochets off an earlier target. The distance to each additional target adds to the total range of the shield, and range penalties apply, but there are no additional penalties for attacking in this manner. Because ricocheting attacks are treated as separate attacks, effects and modifiers that only apply to one attack roll (such as ‹spell/true strike›) only apply to the first attack and not to the others.

::ab[Champion Defense (Ex)]{icon=def l=15 ability="Once per day when she would be reduced to 0 or fewer hit points by damage in combat from a weapon, unarmed, or natural attack, the shield champion can attempt to absorb all the damage with her shield. To use this ability, the shield champion must attempt a Fortitude saving throw, with the DC equal to the damage dealt. If it succeeds, she takes only half damage from the blow; otherwise, she takes full damage. She must be aware of the attack and able to react to it in order to use this ability - if she is denied her Dexterity bonus to AC, she can't use this ability." imp19="She can use this ability twice per day." replace="Maneuver training gained at 15th and 19th levels."}

snakebite_striker.name: Snakebite Striker
description: ## Snakebite Striker
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/88›

With her lightning quickness and guile, a snakebite striker keeps her foes' attention focused on her, because any one of her feints might be an actual attack. By giving up some of a brawler's versatility, she increases her damage potential and exposes opponents to deadly and unexpected strikes.
::cskill{gain=Bluff~Stealth lose=Intimidate}

::ab[Sneak Attack (Ex)]{icon=power ability="The snakebite striker can make a ‹ability/sneak attack›. This is as the rogue ability of the same name." imp1="Her sneak attack damage is +1d6." imp6="Damage becomes +2d6." imp10="Damage becomes +3d6." imp12="Damage becomes +4d6." imp20="Damage becomes +5d6." special="If she gets a sneak attack bonus from another source, the bonuses on damage stack." replace="Martial flexibility"}

::ab[Snake Feint (Ex)]{icon=power l3="A snakebite striker who uses a standard action to move can combine that move with a feint. If she is able to feint as a move action (such as from having the ‹feat/Improved Feint› feat), she can combine a move action to move with her feint." l11="Once per round she can declare her square and one adjacent square as the origin of her attacks until her next turn (allowing her to use one or both squares to determine whether she or allies are flanking an opponent)." l15="She counts an additional adjacent square for this purpose." replace="Maneuver training gained at 3rd and 7th levels."}

::ab[Opportunist (Ex)]{icon=melee l11="Once per round the snakebite striker can make an attack of opportunity against an opponent who has just been struck for damage in melee by another character. This attack counts as an attack of opportunity for that round. She cannot use this ability more than once per round, even if she has the ‹feat/Combat Reflexes› feat or a similar ability." l19="She can use this ability twice per round." replace="Maneuver training gained at 11th and 19th levels."}

steel_breaker.name: Steel-Breaker
description: ## Steel-Breaker
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/88›
The steel-breaker studies destruction and practices it as an art form. She knows every defense has a breaking point, and can shatter those defenses with carefully planned strikes.
::cskill{gain="Knowledge (engineering)" lose="Knowledge (dungeoneering)"}

::ab[Exploit Weakness (Ex)]{icon=boost-lower-def l=5 ability="As a @HLswift_action, a steel-breaker can observe a creature or object to find its weak point by succeeding at a Wisdom check, adding her brawler level against a DC of 10 the object's hardness or the target's CR. If it succeeds, the steel-breaker gains a +2 bonus on attack rolls until the end of her turn, and any attacks she makes until the end of her turn ignore the creature or object's DR or hardness." order=an note="A steel-breaker can instead use this ability as a @HLswift_action to analyze the movements and expressions of one creature within 30 feet, granting a bonus on Sense Motive checks and Reflex saving throws, as well as a dodge bonus to AC against that opponent equal to ‹misc/half› her brawler level until the start of her next turn." replace="Brawler's strike"}

::ab[Sunder Training (Ex)]{icon=melee-boost-def flavor="A steel-breaker receives additional training in sunder combat maneuvers." l3="She gains a +2 bonus when attempting a ‹rule/sunder› combat maneuver checks and a +2 bonus to her CMD when defending against this maneuver." l7="These bonuses increase to +3, and she gains a +2 bonus on ‹rule/disarm› combat maneuver checks and a +2 bonus to her CMD when defending against a disarm maneuver." l11="The sunder bonuses become +4 and the disarm bonuses become +3." l15="These bonuses become +5 and +4, respectively." l19="These bonuses become +6 and +5." alter="Maneuver training"}

strangler.name: Strangler
description: ## Strangler
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/88›

A strangler is trained to choke the life out of her victims with her vise-like grip. Some stranglers are self-taught and are little more than brutish murderers, unhinged sociopaths, or opportunistic alley-bashers. Others are members of murder cults or specialized schools of assassination, trained since the cradle to kill with their bare hands.
::cskill{gain=Stealth}

::ab[Strangle (Ex)]{icon=boost l1="A strangler deals +1d6 ‹ability/sneak attack› damage whenever she succeeds at a ‹rule/grapple› check to damage or pin an opponent. The strangler is always considered flanking her target for the purpose of using this ability." l2="The damage becomes +2d6." l8="The damage becomes +3d6." l15="The damage becomes +4d6." replace="Unarmed strike~brawler's flurry"}

::ab[Practiced Strangler (Ex)]{icon=def-melee l2="When a strangler has the ‹rule/grappled› condition, she does not take a -4 penalty to Dexterity and does not lose her Dexterity bonus to AC." l9="A grappling or grappled strangler still threatens an area and is still able to make attacks of opportunity while grappling or grappled; when pinned, she is not denied her Dexterity bonus and does not take a -4 penalty to Armor Class." replace="AC bonus"}

::ab[Sleeper Hold (Ex)]{icon=power useInc="6~brawler~4" l=4 ability="A strangler can instantly render a pinned opponent unconscious. This works like the *knockout* ability, but requires a successful grapple combat maneuver check against the pinned opponent and does not have to damage the opponent." special="Creatures that do not need to breathe, are immune to critical hits, or are immune to nonlethal damage are immune to this ability." alter=Knockout}

::ab[Neckbreaker (Ex)]{icon=power standard="A strangler can attempt to instantly kill a pinned opponent as a standard action. This works like the *sleeper hold* ability, but imposes a -5 penalty on her grapple combat maneuver check. If the opponent succeeds at its Fortitude save, the strangler deals damage as if she had attempted the grapple check to damage her opponent; if the opponent fails its Fortitude save, it dies." special="Creatures that are immune to critical hits are immune to this ability." imp20="The strangler can attempt this as a @HLmove_action." replace="Awesome blow~improved awesome blow"}

strong_side_boxer.name: Strong-Side Boxer
description: ## Strong-Side Boxer
‹SOURCE Martial Arts Handbook/16›

A strong-side boxer employs a unique fighting style that incorporates a rope or cord wound around her hand. The wrapped hand is known as the strong side. The other hand is kept free to deflect blows, and is known as the shield. The Zenj people of the Mwangi Expanse, who gather for large boxing tournaments, developed this style for their competitions. Many guests and visitors have witnessed these tournaments over the years, and as a result, the style has spread throughout Golarion.

::ab[Shield-Hand (Ex)]{icon=def ability="A strong-side boxer can use her off hand as if it were a ‹eq-armor/buckler›, gaining a natural armor bonus to her AC as long as she does not use her off hand to make attacks. This bonus is equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fifth› her brawler level." special="While using her off hand in this manner, the strong-side boxer treats that hand as a buckler for the purposes of other feats and abilities (though she is also considered to have a free hand)." replace="The brawler's AC bonus"}

::ab[Strong-Side Fist (Ex)]{icon=melee-boost l1="A strong-side boxer can spend 1 minute to wrap one of her hands in rope or cord. The strong-side boxer can carry items or wield weapons in a wrapped hand, but she takes a -2 penalty on Dexterity-based skill checks that requires the use of that hand (such as Disable Device and Sleight of Hand checks). Unwrapping a hand is a @HLmove_action that provokes attacks of opportunity." l3="As a @HLfull-round_action that provokes attacks of opportunity, a strong-side boxer can dip her wrapped hand in resin and then crushed glass or small metal spikes, which remain on her wrapped hand for 1 hour. If she chooses crushed glass, she can deal slashing damage with her unarmed strikes; if she chooses spikes, she can deal piercing damage with her unarmed strikes for the duration. Whether she chooses crushed glass or metal spikes, she deals an additional 1 point of ‹rule/bleed› damage with her unarmed strikes." l7="The bleed damage becomes 1d4." l11="The bleed damage increases to 1d6." l15="The bleed damage becomes 2d4." imp19="The bleed damage is multiplied on a critical hit." special="Bleed damage from this ability does not stack with bleed damage from other sources." replace="Maneuver training"}

::ab[Lead Leg (Ex)]{l=5 order=ai icon=def-boost ability="A strong-side boxer can spend 1 minute to wrap one of her legs in a chain, and can thereafter use the chain-wrapped leg both defensively and offensively. While her leg is wrapped, she is considered to be wearing a ‹eq-armor/chain shirt›, gaining all the appropriate bonuses and penalties. The chain can be enchanted as armor and is treated as ‹rule/masterwork<_a›." immediate="Once per round while wearing the chain, she can apply a +4 circumstance bonus to a ‹rule/trip› attempt." replace="Close weapon mastery"}

turfer.name: Turfer
description: ## Turfer
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/42›
A turfer has a mastery over particular types of terrain.

::ab[Favored Turf (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost-def choice="A turfer chooses a type of terrain from the ranger's ‹ability/favored terrain› list. When in that type of terrain, she gains a +2 bonus on initiative checks and a +1 bonus on combat maneuver checks and to CMD." imp7="The turfer chooses an additional terrain in which to gain these bonuses. In one terrain of her choice (including the one just chosen), her bonus on initiative checks increases by 2, and her bonus on combat maneuver checks and to CMD increases by 1." imp11="She gains another terrain and increases the bonuses in one of her terrains, as above." imp15="She gains a fourth terrain and chooses one to increase bonuses in." imp19="She chooses a fifth terrain and increases the bonuses in one of her terrains." replace="Maneuver training"}

::ab[Terrain Mastery (Su)]{icon=def-boost l4="A turfer gains the benefits of ‹spell/endure elements› and a +10 foot enhancement bonus to her base speed, but only in her favored terrains. In addition, she can move through ‹rule/difficult terrain› in her favored terrains at her normal speed without taking damage or suffering any other impairment, but she can't do so through difficult terrain that has been magically manipulated." l10="Her enhancement bonus to her base speed increases to +20 feet." l16="Her enhancement bonus increases to +30 feet." special="The turfer loses this ability if she wears medium or heavy armor or carries a medium or heavy load." replace=Knockout}

ulfen_beast_wrestler.name: Ulfen Beast-Wrestler
description: ## Ulfen Beast-Wrestler
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Origins/10›

For these mighty grapplers, wrestling normal opponents has lost its challenge - they seek greater targets for glory. Ulfen beast-wrestlers challenge trolls to unarmed combat, and the greatest seek out the great linnorm their lands are known for and wrestle these primeval dragons into submission.

::ab[Beast Training (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost choice="An Ulfen beast-wrestler selects a creature type from the ranger's ‹ability/favored enemies› table. The only humanoid subtype the beast-wrestler can select is giant. She gains a +2 bonus on combat maneuver checks and to her CMD against creatures of the type she selected." imp7="The Ulfen beast-wrestler can select an additional type of creature. In addition, the bonuses against any one type of creature she's chosen (including the one just selected, if so desired) increase by 2." imp11="She chooses another type of creature and increases the bonuses against one type, as above." imp15="She chooses and increases again, as above." imp19="She chooses and increases one more time." special="If a specific creature falls into more than one category, the beast-wrestler's bonuses don't stack; she simply uses whichever bonus is higher." replace="Maneuver training"}

::ab[Beast Defenses (Ex)]{icon=def passive="When facing enemies she selected with beast training, the beast-wrestler gains a bonus to AC equal to ‹misc/half› her beast training bonus against that creature." replace="AC bonus"}

venomfist.name: Venomfist
description: ## Venomfist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/43›
Thanks to alchemical experiments and rigorous study of venomous creatures, a venomfist has toxic unarmed strikes.

::ab[Venomous Strike (Ex)]{icon=down-boost ability="A venomfist's unarmed strikes deal damage as a creature ‹misc/one size category smaller› (1d4 at first level for Medium venomfists). If she hits with her first unarmed strike in a round, the target must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw (DC = 10 + ‹misc/half› the venomfist's brawler level + her Constitution modifier) or take an additional amount of damage equal to the venomfist's Constitution modifier. The venomfist is immune to this toxin." imp4="A target that fails this save must succeed at a second saving throw 1 round later or take the same amount of damage again. This effect repeats as long as the target continues to fail its saving throws, to a maximum number of rounds equal to 1 plus 1 additional round for every 4 brawler levels the venomfist has. Unlike other poisons, multiple doses of a venomfist's poison never stack; the more recent poison effect replaces the older one." imp5="After the venomfist gets 8 hours of rest, she can choose a secondary effect for her venom to impose. She can choose ‹misc/fatigued›, ‹misc/shaken›, or ‹misc/sickened›. A creature that fails its saving throw against her venom also gains the chosen condition until it succeeds at a save against the venom or until the venom's duration ends. Once a creature succeeds at its save against the poison, it becomes immune to the secondary condition for 24 hours, but the attack still deals the extra damage." imp10="When the venomfist chooses the condition her venom imposes, she can also cause her venom to deal ‹rule/ability «score »damage› each round instead of hit point damage. She chooses Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution, and her venom deals 1d3 points of ability score damage each round. In addition, she adds ‹misc/blinded›, ‹misc/exhausted›, and ‹misc/staggered› to the list of secondary effects she can choose for her venom." imp16="The venomfist's venom is particularly potent. If it fails the initial save, the target must succeed at two consecutive saves before being cured of the venom, though if the first save is successful, the secondary effect ends and the creature is immune to the secondary effects of the venomfist's poison for 24 hours. In addition, the venomfist adds ‹misc/dazed› and ‹misc/stunned› to the list of secondary effects she can choose for her venom." special="This is a poison effect. The venomfist can't suppress it, but she affects only those she damages with her unarmed strikes (not those she merely touches)." alter="Unarmed strike" replace="Knockout~close weapon mastery"}

verdant_grappler.name: Verdant Grappler
description: ## Verdant Grappler
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/43›
By accepting spirits of nature into her soul, a verdant grappler can entangle her foes with tangled undergrowth.

::ab[Green Grasp (Su)]{icon=power l2="A verdant grappler gains ‹feat/Improved Grapple› as a bonus feat." l3="She must choose ‹rule/grapple› as her first combat maneuver for maneuver training. When she pins a creature, she can cause roots and vines to burst from the ground (no matter the surrounding terrain), allowing the verdant grappler to attempt to tie up the creature without needing to retrieve rope or use a hand to hold the rope; as usual, a tied-up creature is ‹rule/pinned› and not helpless." special="The verdant grappler must remain within 30 feet of the tied-up creature, or the vines automatically release it. She can tie up only one creature at a time with *green grasp.*" replace="Bonus feat gained at 2nd level" alter="Maneuver training"}

::ab[Thorny Embrace (Su)]{icon=boost l5="When a verdant grappler uses *green grasp* to tie up a creature, she can have the roots and vines grow vicious thorns that automatically damage the tied-up creature each round at the start of the verdant grappler's turn. The thorns deal damage equal to the base unarmed strike damage of a brawler 4 levels lower than the verdant grappler." replace="Close weapon mastery"}

::ab[Phytological Anatomy (Su)]{icon=power-def l=11 passive="The verdant grappler transforms partially into a plant. She gains a +2 bonus on saving throws against mind-affecting, paralysis, poison, polymorph, sleep, and stunning effects." replace="The combat feat gained at 11th level"}

wild_child.name: Wild Child
description: ## Wild Child
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/89›

The wild child works with his sworn animal friend to conquer the challenges that lay before them. This kinship could come from being lost in the wilderness and raised by animals or growing up with an exotic pet.
::cskill{gain=Heal}

::ab[Animal Companion (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A wild child forms a bond with a loyal companion that accompanies the wild child on his adventures. A wild child can begin play with any of the ‹ability/animal«s<_companion› available to a druid. The wild child uses his brawler level as his effective druid level for determining the abilities of his ‹sidekick/animal companion›." replace="Bonus combat feats gained at 2nd, 8th, 14th, and 20th levels."}

::ab[Hunter's Tricks]{icon=power l=5 ability="A wild child can expend a use of martial flexibility to use a *hunter's trick* from the ‹arc-ranger/ranger »skirmisher« archetype›. Each time he activates this ability, the wild child can use a different hunter trick. He cannot choose any tricks that rely on ranged attacks. Activating this ability is not an action, but using the trick might require the wild child to use an action of a different type." replace="Close weapon mastery"}

::ab[Maneuver Training (Ex)]{icon=power ability="Whenever the wild child chooses a new maneuver for this ability, his animal companion also learns a trick to make use of this combat maneuver. For example, if the wild child chooses maneuver training (dirty trick), his animal companion also learns a trick that allows it to use the dirty trick combat maneuver. He cannot choose any tricks that rely on ranged attacks." special="This bonus trick doesn't count against the animal companion's total tricks known and does not take any time or checks to train. Should the wild child gain a new animal companion (such as if the previous one dies), this new animal companion begins with the same number of bonus tricks." alter="Maneuver training"}

::ab[Wild Tricks (Ex)]{icon=power flavor="The wild child has learned a number of tricks to aid his allies and his animal companion, as well as to hinder his opponents." l5="The wild child learns one *hunter's trick* from the ranger skirmisher archetype. He cannot choose a trick that relies on ranged attacks." l11="The wild child learns a second hunter's trick, with the same restrictions." l17="The wild child learns a third hunter's trick, with the same restrictions." special="The wild child can use these tricks a number of times per day equal to ‹misc/half› his brawler level + his Constitution modifier. This ability otherwise follows the rules of the hunter's tricks ability, including all action costs." replace="Bonus combat feats gained at 5th, 11th, and 17th levels."}

winding_path_renegade.name: Winding Path Renegade
description: ## Winding Path Renegade
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/105;Advanced Class Origins/11›

In the sacred text Unbinding the Fetters, Irori teaches that the path to transcendence can be straight or twisted. Those who follow the straight path adhere to the traditions and techniques perfected by others who have traveled before them, while those who seek the twisted path abandon tradition and follow their own unique routes to self-perfection. Both paths can lead to the same place, but those who follow the twisted path must take care to not lose their way.

Winding path renegades epitomize those who have chosen the twisted path. These brawlers initially studied at the ‹rule/Houses of Perfection› in Jalmeray, but while they grasped the mechanical aspects of the forms, they lacked the mental discipline required by the masters of the school. Thus, they have withdrawn from their monasteries - some peacefully, some not - to find their own paths.

::ab[School Focus (Ex)]{head l=2 icon=power ability="A winding path renegade continues the training she left behind, gaining the benefits of the mystery taught at her old monastery. As the winding path renegade progresses in her self-guided training, she gains a deeper understanding of her chosen monastery's mystery, which grants her new powers." replace="Bonus feats gained at 2nd, 8th, and 14th levels."}

::ab[Mystery of Unblinking Flame (Su)]{next icon=boost-melee-def l2="The winding path renegade increases her speed by 10 feet (this is treated as an enhancement bonus). If she uses her *monk moves* class ability to gain fast movement, the abilities stack with each other." l8="If the winding path renegade begins her turn with the ‹rule/grappled› condition, she can deal 2d6 points of fire damage to all other creatures in the grapple as a @HLfree_action." l14="The winding path renegade becomes as difficult to contain as a flame. Once per day as a @HLfull-round_action, she can transform into liquid flame, instantly escaping all bonds as if under the effect of ‹spell/freedom of movement› and moving up to her speed. When moving as part of this ability, she can move through creatures' squares freely and doesn't provoke attacks of opportunity. She deals 10d6 points of fire damage to any creature whose square she enters during this movement. A successful Reflex saving throw (DC = 10 + ‹misc/half› her brawler level + her Dexterity bonus) halves this damage."}

::ab[Mystery of Unfolding Wind (Su)]{next icon=boost-power l2="The winding path renegade adds 10 to the range increments of ranged weapons she wields and gains ‹feat/Deflect Arrows› as a bonus feat." l8="She can harness the power of the wind to make enormous leaps. As a @HLmove_action, she can leap without attempting an Acrobatics check, jumping any distance up to her speed (upward movement counts as double, as when flying). She can leap in this way a number of times per day equal to her class level." l14="When using the Deflect Arrows feat, the winding path renegade can send one projectile back at her attacker, using the same attack roll and damage roll the attacker rolled against her. Even if the deflected attack had more than one projectile (as with ‹feat/Manyshot›), the winding path renegade sends only one projectile back at the attacker. The others are deflected as normal."}

::ab[Mystery of Untwisting Iron (Su)]{next icon=boost l2="Metal weapons and armor in the winding path renegade's possession are treated as ‹rule/masterwork<_w› unless they have the ‹misc/broken› condition." l8="The winding path renegade gains a bonus equal to half her level on Craft checks involving metal, and she gains the benefits of the ‹feat/Master Craftsman› and ‹feat/Craft Magic Arms and Armor› feats when crafting items made of metal." l14="Any weapon the winding path renegade wields is treated as ‹eq-material/adamantine›."}

::ab[Monk Moves (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A winding path renegade can use her martial flexibility ability to gain the benefits of certain ‹class/monk› abilities as well as combat feats. Each ability counts as one combat feat for this purpose, and the winding path renegade is treated as a monk of a level equal to her brawler level for the purpose of determining the effects of these abilities." special="The winding path renegade gains access to these abilities at the levels listed on the Monk Moves table below. She does not gain a ki pool, and she can't use powers that require ki. The winding path renegade must be wearing light or no armor to benefit from this ability." replace="AC bonus"}
| Monk Ability | Level Gained |
| @ripple[ability/Evasion] | 4th |
| Fast movement | 4th |
| Slow fall | 4th |
| High jump | 5th |
| @ripple[ability/Improved evasion] | 9th |
